touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Ravi Martin at PineApps. No answer, left voicemail introducing AskElephant's call recording and coaching platform.
Cold email to Ravi introducing AskElephant's conversation intelligence platform. Highlighted ROI for PropTech sales teams. Ravi responded expressing interest in learning more about coaching features.

questions_asked.question: How does your coaching functionality compare to what Gong offers?
questions_asked.answer: Walked through our AI-powered coaching scorecards and real-time feedback engine, which provides more actionable insights than Gong's post-call analysis.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sharing case study from another PropTech company. Ravi confirmed interest and proposed times for discovery call next week.
next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for next Tuesday

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Ravi Martin, Head of RevOps. Discussed current pain points with coaching consistency across their 14-person sales team. Ravi mentioned they're evaluating Gong but concerned about price. Explored use case around automating call reviews and identifying coaching opportunities.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the evaluation right now?
questions_asked.answer: Our VP Sales wants better visibility into deal health and rep performance, especially with newer hires.

questions_asked.question: Who else is involved in this decision?
questions_asked.answer: Mainly me and our VP Sales. I'll probably need to get CFO sign-off on the budget.

questions_asked.question: What's your typical pricing model for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: Explained seat-based pricing with automation tiers. Ballpark for 14 seats with unlimited automation would be around $14-15K annually.

objections: Need to stay within budget—Gong quoted us pretty high

touches.ai_summary: Quick follow-up discovery call to clarify integration requirements. Ravi confirmed they use Salesforce and need seamless sync. Discussed timeline—hoping to have something in place before Q2 ends.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation typically take?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 1-2 weeks for Salesforce integration and team onboarding.

questions_asked.question: Have you gotten the VP Sales on board with moving forward?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, she's supportive. Just need to finalize budget approval.

next_steps: Schedule technical evaluation with RevOps team

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation session with Ravi and two RevOps analysts. Walked through platform capabilities, automation workflows, and coaching dashboards. Team engaged and asked detailed questions about Salesforce integration and data security. Ravi emphasized need to beat Gong's pricing to move forward.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the coaching rubrics for our sales methodology?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, fully customizable. Walked through examples of tailored scorecards for PropTech discovery calls.

questions_asked.question: What's your SOC 2 status?
questions_asked.answer: SOC 2 Type II certified, shared compliance documentation.

questions_asked.question: What would make this a no-brainer decision for your team?
questions_asked.answer: Pricing is the biggest factor. If you can come in meaningfully below Gong, we'll move fast.

objections: Gong has more brand recognition with our exec team
next_steps: Loop in VP Sales for exec alignment call

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with formal proposal. Included 14 seats, unlimited automation tier, $10,300 ACV (30% discount to stay competitive with Gong). Ravi responded positively and requested exec meeting to present to VP Sales and CFO.

questions_asked.question: Can you join a call with our VP Sales to walk through the ROI?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, let's find a time that works for everyone next week.

next_steps: Schedule exec meeting with VP Sales and CFO

touches.ai_summary: Exec meeting was rescheduled last minute due to VP Sales conflict. Ravi apologized and proposed new time for later in the week. Brief check-in confirmed continued interest.

questions_asked.question: Is there anything blocking on your end that we should address before the rescheduled meeting?
questions_asked.answer: No, just scheduling chaos. VP Sales is still on board.

touches.ai_summary: Quick pricing clarification call. Ravi asked if we could adjust payment terms to quarterly instead of annual upfront. Explained billing flexibility and confirmed the $10,300 ACV holds.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly billing?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can structure quarterly payments with a small admin fee.

next_steps: Update proposal with quarterly payment option

touches.ai_summary: Rescheduled exec meeting with Ravi. VP Sales was unavailable again, so call was only with Ravi and one RevOps analyst. Discussed ROI projections and implementation timeline. Ravi confirmed he has internal buy-in but needs to finalize contract terms with finance.

questions_asked.question: When can we get the VP Sales and CFO on a call together?
questions_asked.answer: It's been tough to coordinate. I think I can get approval directly from CFO if we keep the pricing where it is.

questions_asked.question: What's your standard contract length?
questions_asked.answer: Typically 12 months, but we can discuss flexibility.

objections: CFO is pushing back on adding any new software spend this quarter

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Ravi focused on finalizing contract terms. Ravi pushed for an out-clause after 6 months and asked about further discount. Held firm on pricing given the 30% discount already applied. Ravi agreed to present final terms to CFO but mentioned Gong is still in the running and they may need another week to decide.

questions_asked.question: Can we add a 6-month exit clause?
questions_asked.answer: We typically don't offer that for annual contracts, but I can check with leadership.

questions_asked.question: What would it take to close this week?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, I need CFO to sign off and we're still comparing your proposal against Gong's. Probably another 5-7 days.

questions_asked.question: If we commit today, can you go any lower on price?
questions_asked.answer: We're already at 30% off to stay competitive. That's the best we can do.

objections: Gong's proposal includes more free onboarding support
CFO wants proof this won't just be shelfware
next_steps: Follow up in 3 days for CFO decision
